phase.name: Cross-Cutting Fixes: Regex Ban and Deployment Config
phase.description: Fix all banned regex violations (CLAUDE.md Rule #16) and the missing post_training entry in FLOW_WORK_POOL_MAP. These are independent of the main volume audit and can be done first as they unblock analysis flow and eliminate silent correctness bugs.

task.title: Fix regex violation in analysis_flow.py — replace re.compile with rsplit()
task.github_issue: #369 (regex ban is a sub-constraint of the audit)
task.current_state: src/minivess/orchestration/flows/analysis_flow.py line 1033: _FOLD_RE = re.compile(r"^(.+)_fold(\d+)$") import re is at the top of analysis_flow.py (line ~22). This is an explicit violation of CLAUDE.md Rule #16 and the regex ban metalearning doc. Metric names like "val_dice_fold0" are structured strings that must be parsed with str methods, not regex.
task.target_state: - import re is removed from analysis_flow.py - _FOLD_RE is replaced by a helper function using str.rsplit("_fold", 1) - All uses of _FOLD_RE.match() are replaced with the str.rsplit() approach - The file passes `uv run ruff check` and `uv run mypy`
task.tdd_spec: RED phase — write tests/v2/unit/test_analysis_flow_no_regex.py: test_fold_name_parse_basic: assert parse_fold_metric("val_dice_fold0") == ("val_dice", 0) test_fold_name_parse_multi_fold: assert parse_fold_metric("compound_masd_cldice_fold2") == ("compound_masd_cldice", 2) test_fold_name_parse_no_fold: parse_fold_metric("val_loss") raises ValueError (no _fold suffix) test_analysis_flow_no_re_import: ast.parse(source) walk finds no import re or import regex node GREEN phase: Add parse_fold_metric(name: str) -> tuple[str, int] function using str.rsplit("_fold", 1) Replace all _FOLD_RE.match() calls with parse_fold_metric() Remove import re from analysis_flow.py
task.acceptance_criteria: - grep -n "import re" src/minivess/orchestration/flows/analysis_flow.py returns nothing - All existing analysis_flow tests continue to pass - New test_fold_name_parse_* tests pass - ruff check reports no violations

task.title: Fix regex violation in duckdb_extraction.py — replace re.match with str.split()
task.current_state: src/minivess/pipeline/duckdb_extraction.py line 364: re.match(r"eval_fold(\d+)_(.+)", metric_name) Parses metric names like "eval_fold0_val_dice" to extract fold index and metric name. This is structured string parsing that must use str.split() per CLAUDE.md Rule #16.
task.target_state: - import re is removed from duckdb_extraction.py (or re is no longer used for metric parsing) - Metric name parsing uses str.partition("_fold") + str.split("_", 1) or equivalent - All 5 components of the metric name (prefix, fold_index, metric_name) are correctly extracted - The file passes ruff check and mypy
task.tdd_spec: RED phase — write tests/v2/unit/test_duckdb_extraction_no_regex.py: test_metric_parse_eval_fold: parse_eval_fold_metric("eval_fold0_val_dice") == (0, "val_dice") test_metric_parse_multi_digit_fold: parse_eval_fold_metric("eval_fold12_compound_masd") == (12, "compound_masd") test_metric_parse_non_fold: parse_eval_fold_metric("train_loss") returns None (not an eval fold metric) test_duckdb_no_re_import: ast.parse(source) walk finds no import re for metric parsing GREEN phase: Add parse_eval_fold_metric(name: str) -> tuple[int, str] | None Using: if "_fold" not in name: return None _, rest = name.partition("_fold") fold_str, _, metric = rest.partition("_") return int(fold_str), metric Replace re.match() call at line 364 with parse_eval_fold_metric()
task.acceptance_criteria: - grep -n "re.match" src/minivess/pipeline/duckdb_extraction.py returns nothing - Existing DuckDB extraction tests continue to pass - New metric parsing tests pass

task.title: Add post_training to FLOW_WORK_POOL_MAP in deployments.py
task.current_state: src/minivess/orchestration/deployments.py: FLOW_WORK_POOL_MAP does NOT contain "post_training" FLOW_IMAGE_MAP DOES contain "post_training": "minivess-post-training:latest" get_flow_deployment_config("post_training") returns cpu-pool (default fallback) but FLOW_IMAGE_MAP.get("post_training") returns the correct image. The inconsistency means the deployment config is partially wrong.
task.target_state: - FLOW_WORK_POOL_MAP["post_training"] = "cpu-pool" - get_flow_deployment_config("post_training") returns correct work_pool + image - "annotation" is also added to both maps
task.tdd_spec: RED phase — tests/v2/unit/test_deployments_config.py (add to existing or create): test_all_flows_in_work_pool_map: all flow names in FLOW_IMAGE_MAP are also in FLOW_WORK_POOL_MAP test_post_training_config: get_flow_deployment_config("post_training")["work_pool"] == "cpu-pool" test_post_training_image: get_flow_deployment_config("post_training")["image"] == "minivess-post-training:latest" test_annotation_config: get_flow_deployment_config("annotation")["work_pool"] == "cpu-pool" GREEN phase: Add "post_training": "cpu-pool" to FLOW_WORK_POOL_MAP Add "annotation": "cpu-pool" to FLOW_WORK_POOL_MAP Add "annotation": "minivess-annotation:latest" to FLOW_IMAGE_MAP
task.acceptance_criteria: - All flows listed in trigger.py._DEFAULT_FLOWS are in FLOW_WORK_POOL_MAP - FLOW_IMAGE_MAP and FLOW_WORK_POOL_MAP have identical key sets - Deployment config tests pass

phase.name: Docker Volume Audit — docker-compose.flows.yml
phase.description: Add missing volume declarations to all flow services. This is a surgical edit to docker-compose.flows.yml that enables all flows to persist their outputs across container restarts. Also adds the missing acquisition service. Every artifact that must survive container exit must have an explicit mount.

task.title: Add acquisition service and all missing volume mounts to docker-compose.flows.yml
task.current_state:
deployment/docker-compose.flows.yml: - acquisition service: MISSING ENTIRELY (no service definition) - data service: only data_cache:/app/data (missing mlruns, splits) - train service: data_cache + checkpoint_cache (missing mlruns, logs, splits) - post_training service: checkpoint_cache:ro (missing outputs, mlruns, data for calibration) - analyze service: NO VOLUMES AT ALL - deploy service: NO VOLUMES AT ALL - dashboard service: NO VOLUMES AT ALL - qa service: NO VOLUMES AT ALL
Named volumes defined: only data_cache and checkpoint_cache Missing named volumes: mlruns_data, raw_data, outputs_analysis, outputs_deploy, outputs_dashboard, outputs_qa, bentoml_store, logs_data, configs_splits, post_training_out
task.target_state:
Full volume specification per service (see intermedia-plan-synthesis.md Part 2.1):
acquisition: - raw_data:/app/data/raw (rw) - logs_data:/app/logs (rw)
data: - raw_data:/app/data/raw:ro - data_cache:/app/data/processed (rw) - configs_splits:/app/configs/splits (rw) - mlruns_data:/app/mlruns (rw)
train: - data_cache:/app/data:ro - configs_splits:/app/configs/splits:ro - checkpoint_cache:/app/checkpoints (rw) - mlruns_data:/app/mlruns (rw) - logs_data:/app/logs (rw)
post_training: - checkpoint_cache:/app/checkpoints:ro - data_cache:/app/data:ro - post_training_out:/app/outputs/post_training (rw) - mlruns_data:/app/mlruns (rw)
analyze: - data_cache:/app/data:ro - checkpoint_cache:/app/checkpoints:ro - configs_splits:/app/configs/splits:ro - mlruns_data:/app/mlruns (rw) - outputs_analysis:/app/outputs/analysis (rw)
deploy: - checkpoint_cache:/app/checkpoints:ro - mlruns_data:/app/mlruns:ro - outputs_deploy:/app/outputs/deploy (rw) - bentoml_store:/home/minivess/bentoml (rw)
dashboard: - mlruns_data:/app/mlruns:ro - outputs_analysis:/app/outputs/analysis:ro - outputs_dashboard:/app/outputs/dashboard (rw)
qa: - mlruns_data:/app/mlruns:ro - outputs_dashboard:/app/outputs/dashboard (rw)
All new named volumes declared in the top-level volumes: section. acquisition service added with Dockerfile.acquisition and correct env vars.
task.tdd_spec: RED phase — tests/v2/integration/test_docker_compose_volumes.py: test_acquisition_service_exists: parse docker-compose.flows.yml with yaml.safe_load(), assert "acquisition" in services test_acquisition_has_raw_data_mount: assert any(v.startswith("raw_data:") for v in services["acquisition"]["volumes"]) test_analyze_has_mlruns_mount: assert any("mlruns_data" in v for v in services["analyze"]["volumes"]) test_analyze_has_outputs_mount: assert any("outputs_analysis" in v for v in services["analyze"]["volumes"]) test_deploy_has_outputs_mount: assert any("outputs_deploy" in v for v in services["deploy"]["volumes"]) test_deploy_has_bentoml_mount: assert any("bentoml_store" in v for v in services["deploy"]["volumes"]) test_dashboard_has_outputs_mount: assert any("outputs_dashboard" in v for v in services["dashboard"]["volumes"]) test_qa_has_mlruns_mount: assert any("mlruns_data" in v for v in services["qa"]["volumes"]) test_all_referenced_volumes_declared: all volume names in services["*"]["volumes"] appear in top-level volumes test_train_has_no_tmp_paths: no volume in services["train"]["volumes"] contains "/tmp" NOTE: All parsing uses yaml.safe_load() — NO regex, NO string scanning GREEN phase: Edit deployment/docker-compose.flows.yml with all required volume declarations Add acquisition service Add all 10 new named volumes to the volumes: section Verify with: docker compose -f deployment/docker-compose.flows.yml config
task.acceptance_criteria: - `docker compose -f deployment/docker-compose.flows.yml config` exits 0 - All integration tests in test_docker_compose_volumes.py pass - No service has zero volumes declared - No volume path contains "/tmp" - All volume names referenced in services appear in the top-level volumes section

phase.name: Fix Ephemeral Checkpoint Directory in Training
phase.description:
The root cause of checkpoint loss on container exit: scripts/train_monitored.py:673 creates checkpoint_dir via tempfile.mkdtemp(). This is /tmp/tmpXXXXXX inside Docker, which is ephemeral and lost on container exit. The fix reads CHECKPOINT_DIR from env.
This is the most impactful single-line fix in the entire codebase.

task.title: Replace tempfile.mkdtemp() with env-var-configured checkpoint path in train_monitored.py
task.current_state:
scripts/train_monitored.py line 673: checkpoint_dir = Path(tempfile.mkdtemp()) / f"fold_{fold_id}" import tempfile at line 110.
This means: - Inside Docker: checkpoints go to /tmp/tmpXXX/fold_0/ — lost on container exit - Resume (--resume flag) always fails because there is nothing to resume from - MLflow artifact upload of checkpoints is never triggered (the checkpoint is gone)
The checkpoint_cache volume is mounted at /app/checkpoints but never written to.
task.target_state: - import tempfile removed from scripts/train_monitored.py (or no longer used for checkpoints) - checkpoint_dir = Path(os.environ.get("CHECKPOINT_DIR", "/app/checkpoints")) / f"fold_{fold_id}" - The /app/checkpoints path maps to the checkpoint_cache named volume - Resume logic correctly reads from CHECKPOINT_DIR on restart - CHECKPOINT_DIR env var documented in deployment/docker-compose.flows.yml train service
task.tdd_spec: RED phase — tests/v2/unit/test_train_monitored_checkpoint_dir.py: test_checkpoint_dir_from_env: with CHECKPOINT_DIR=/test/ckpt set in env, the resolved checkpoint_dir.parts includes "test" and "ckpt" test_checkpoint_dir_not_in_tmp: the resolved checkpoint_dir does not start with /tmp test_checkpoint_dir_includes_fold_id: checkpoint_dir.name == "fold_0" for fold_id=0 test_no_tempfile_import: ast.parse(open("scripts/train_monitored.py").read()) walk finds no import for "tempfile" module (or tempfile is not used for checkpoint_dir) GREEN phase: Replace line 673 of scripts/train_monitored.py: OLD: checkpoint_dir = Path(tempfile.mkdtemp()) / f"fold_{fold_id}" NEW: checkpoint_dir = Path(os.environ.get("CHECKPOINT_DIR", "/app/checkpoints")) / f"fold_{fold_id}" Remove `import tempfile` if no longer used elsewhere Add CHECKPOINT_DIR to docker-compose.flows.yml train environment section
task.acceptance_criteria: - checkpoint_dir never resolves to a /tmp path when CHECKPOINT_DIR env var is set - import tempfile is not present in train_monitored.py (or is clearly not used for checkpoints) - After container restart, checkpoints written before restart are still present (verified by integration test that writes and reads across simulated restart)
task.files_to_modify: scripts/train_monitored.py (line 673 and import section) deployment/docker-compose.flows.yml (add CHECKPOINT_DIR to train environment) tests/v2/unit/test_train_monitored_checkpoint_dir.py (new)

phase.name: Training Flow — Implement Real Execution Body
phase.description:
The training_flow() in train_flow.py is a stub that returns {"status": "configured"} without executing any training. This is the central violation of Issue #367. The flow must call SegmentationTrainer.fit() directly, not delegate to train_monitored.py.
This task is the largest single implementation task in the entire plan.

task.title: Implement training_flow() body — replace stub with real SegmentationTrainer.fit() call
task.current_state:
src/minivess/orchestration/flows/train_flow.py: run_training() task returns: {"status": "configured", "message": "Training flow configured. Use scripts/train_monitored.py for execution."} training_flow() calls run_training() and returns the stub dict.
The flow: - Does NOT open an MLflow run - Does NOT read fold splits from /app/configs/splits - Does NOT call SegmentationTrainer.fit() - Does NOT write checkpoints to the checkpoint volume - Does NOT log upstream_data_run_id tag - Does NOT call FlowContract.log_flow_completion()
task.target_state:
training_flow() must: 1. Open MLflow run in "minivess_training" experiment via ExperimentTracker 2. Read upstream data run ID via FlowContract.find_upstream_run() 3. Load TrainingConfig from experiment YAML (not hardcoded defaults) 4. Load fold splits from Path(os.environ["SPLITS_DIR"]) / "splits.json" 5. For each fold: a. Build DataLoaders from the fold split b. Build ModelAdapter (DynUNet or configured model) c. Build SegmentationTrainer d. Call trainer.fit(train_loader, val_loader, checkpoint_dir=Path(os.environ["CHECKPOINT_DIR"]) / f"fold_{fold_id}") e. Log fold results to MLflow 6. Tag training run with upstream_data_run_id, flow_name="train" 7. Call FlowContract.log_flow_completion() 8. Return TrainingFlowResult dataclass (not a plain dict stub)
run_training() task must be split into multiple focused tasks: - load_fold_splits_task() - build_data_loaders_task() - train_one_fold_task() [calls SegmentationTrainer.fit()] - log_fold_results_task()
task.tdd_spec:
RED phase — tests/v2/unit/test_train_flow_real.py: test_training_flow_returns_real_result: training_flow() with minimal config returns TrainingFlowResult (not a dict with status="configured") test_training_flow_opens_mlflow_run: after training_flow() runs (with mock trainer), mlflow.search_runs("minivess_training") finds a run with flow_name tag test_training_flow_reads_splits: with a mock splits.json at SPLITS_DIR, training_flow() uses it (not hardcoded fold assignment) test_training_flow_checkpoint_dir_not_tmp: the checkpoint_dir used in train_one_fold_task does not start with /tmp test_training_flow_logs_upstream_run_id: MLflow run has "upstream_data_run_id" tag test_train_flow_no_argparse_namespace: ast.parse() walk on train_flow.py finds no argparse.Namespace construction (the stub pattern) test_run_training_task_deprecated_message: if run_training() stub is called directly, it logs a deprecation warning and raises NotImplementedError
RED phase — tests/v2/integration/test_train_flow_with_monai.py: test_train_flow_one_fold_cpu: training_flow() with debug=True, num_folds=1, max_epochs=1 completes without error on CPU test_train_flow_creates_checkpoint: after training_flow() debug run, checkpoint file exists in CHECKPOINT_DIR/fold_0/ test_train_flow_mlflow_has_epoch_metrics: MLflow run has val_loss metric at step=1
GREEN phase: Rewrite src/minivess/orchestration/flows/train_flow.py Add TrainingFlowResult dataclass Add load_fold_splits_task(), build_data_loaders_task(), train_one_fold_task(), log_fold_results_task() Wire FlowContract into training_flow() DO NOT delete run_training() — add deprecation warning + NotImplementedError
task.acceptance_criteria: - training_flow(debug=True, num_folds=1, max_epochs=1) completes without error - At least one MLflow run exists in "minivess_training" after flow completes - The run has val_loss metric at step=1 (epoch-level logging works) - The run has upstream_data_run_id tag (or "no_upstream" if no data run found) - Checkpoint file exists at CHECKPOINT_DIR/fold_0/best_val_loss.pth - grep "Use scripts/train_monitored.py" src/minivess/orchestration/flows/train_flow.py returns nothing (stub message removed) - The string "configured" does not appear as the only status in training_flow() output

task.title: Fix SkyPilot train_generic.yaml — replace python scripts/ with prefect deployment run
task.current_state: deployment/skypilot/train_generic.yaml line 50: run: | cd /app uv run python scripts/train_monitored.py \ --compute ${COMPUTE} \ --loss ${LOSS_NAME} \ ... This invokes a standalone Python script — explicitly banned per Issue #367 and metalearning 2026-03-06-standalone-script-antipattern.md.
task.target_state:
deployment/skypilot/train_generic.yaml run section: run: | cd /app prefect deployment run 'training-flow/default' \ --params "{\"loss_name\": \"${LOSS_NAME}\", \"model_family\": \"${MODEL_FAMILY}\", \"compute\": \"${COMPUTE}\", \"experiment_name\": \"${EXPERIMENT_NAME}\"}"
The setup section must install prefect and configure PREFECT_API_URL. The SkyPilot job must have access to the Prefect server (via VPN or public URL). If Prefect server is not accessible from spot instance, use PREFECT_DISABLED=0 and point at the on-prem server IP via PREFECT_API_URL env var.
task.tdd_spec: RED phase — tests/v2/unit/test_skypilot_configs.py: test_train_generic_no_python_script_invocation: yaml.safe_load(train_generic.yaml), assert "scripts/train_monitored.py" not in config["run"] test_train_generic_uses_prefect_run: assert "prefect deployment run" in config["run"] test_train_hpo_no_python_script: yaml.safe_load(train_hpo_sweep.yaml), assert "scripts/run_hpo.py" not in config["run"] NOTE: All YAML parsing uses yaml.safe_load() — no regex GREEN phase: Edit deployment/skypilot/train_generic.yaml run section Edit deployment/skypilot/train_hpo_sweep.yaml run section (see T-08) Verify setup section installs prefect: pip install uv && uv sync --no-dev
task.acceptance_criteria: - "scripts/train_monitored.py" does not appear in any SkyPilot run section - "prefect deployment run" appears in train_generic.yaml run section - YAML files are syntactically valid (yaml.safe_load() parses without error)

task.title: Fix SkyPilot train_hpo_sweep.yaml — replace python scripts/ with prefect deployment run
task.current_state: deployment/skypilot/train_hpo_sweep.yaml line 37: run: | cd /app uv run python scripts/run_hpo.py \ --config ${HPO_CONFIG} ... Same violation as T-07: standalone Python script invocation. Note: hpo_flow.py does not yet exist as a Prefect flow.
task.target_state:
Either: (a) Create src/minivess/orchestration/flows/hpo_flow.py as a proper Prefect flow wrapping HPOEngine, and invoke via prefect deployment run 'hpo-flow/default' OR (b) Wrap run_hpo.py in an Optuna-Prefect integration where each trial is a Prefect task submitted to the gpu-pool
The SkyPilot run section must use prefect deployment run.
task.tdd_spec: RED phase — tests/v2/unit/test_hpo_flow.py (new, if creating hpo_flow.py): test_hpo_flow_decorated: hpo_flow function has @flow decorator test_hpo_flow_returns_study: hpo_flow(n_trials=1) returns dict with "best_params" key test_hpo_flow_no_python_script: ast.parse(hpo_flow.py source) finds no subprocess.run call with "scripts/run_hpo.py" GREEN phase: Create src/minivess/orchestration/flows/hpo_flow.py Update deployment/skypilot/train_hpo_sweep.yaml run section
task.acceptance_criteria: - "scripts/run_hpo.py" does not appear in any SkyPilot run section - hpo_flow.py has a @flow decorated function - YAML is syntactically valid

phase.name: Post-Training Flow — Volume and Output Path Fixes

task.title: Fix post_training_flow output_dir relative path — use env var for /app/outputs/post_training
task.current_state: src/minivess/orchestration/flows/post_training_flow.py line 152: if output_dir is None: output_dir = Path("outputs/post_training") Inside Docker, this resolves to /app/outputs/post_training which has no volume mount. All SWA, merging, calibration artifacts are lost on container exit.
task.target_state: if output_dir is None: output_dir = Path(os.environ.get("POST_TRAINING_OUTPUT_DIR", "/app/outputs/post_training")) The /app/outputs/post_training path maps to the post_training_out named volume. Plugin output sub-paths (output_dir / plugin_name) are all within the mounted volume.
task.tdd_spec: RED phase — tests/v2/unit/test_post_training_output_dir.py: test_output_dir_from_env: with POST_TRAINING_OUTPUT_DIR=/test/pt set, post_training_flow() uses Path("/test/pt") as output root test_output_dir_not_relative: resolved output_dir.is_absolute() is True test_plugin_subdirs_within_output: for each plugin, output subdir is a child of output_dir test_no_hardcoded_relative_path: ast.parse() walk on post_training_flow.py finds no Path("outputs/post_training") literal construction GREEN phase: Add import os at top of post_training_flow.py (after from __future__ import annotations) Replace line 152 with env-var-based path Update docker-compose.flows.yml post_training service environment section to add POST_TRAINING_OUTPUT_DIR=/app/outputs/post_training
task.acceptance_criteria: - POST_TRAINING_OUTPUT_DIR env var controls output path - Default resolves to /app/outputs/post_training (absolute) - No relative Path("outputs/...") literals in post_training_flow.py

task.title: Add MLflow run logging to post_training_flow — log plugin metrics with post_ prefix
task.current_state: post_training_flow() returns plugin results as a Python dict but does NOT: - Open an MLflow run - Log plugin metrics to MLflow - Read upstream_training_run_id from FlowContract - Call FlowContract.log_flow_completion() Plugin execution results (SWA model paths, calibration metrics) are ephemeral.
task.target_state: post_training_flow() must: 1. Open MLflow run in "minivess_training" experiment 2. Read upstream training run ID via FlowContract.find_upstream_run() 3. Log each plugin's metrics with "post_{plugin_name}_" prefix 4. Log model paths as MLflow artifacts (or tags) 5. Call FlowContract.log_flow_completion() 6. Return enriched result dict with mlflow_run_id
task.tdd_spec: RED phase — tests/v2/unit/test_post_training_mlflow.py: test_post_training_opens_mlflow_run: after post_training_flow() with mock plugins, mlflow.search_runs("minivess_training") finds a run with flow_name="post_training" test_post_training_logs_plugin_metrics: MLflow run has metrics with "post_" prefix test_post_training_logs_upstream_run_id: run has "upstream_training_run_id" tag test_post_training_result_has_run_id: returned dict contains "mlflow_run_id" key GREEN phase: Add ExperimentTracker and FlowContract imports to post_training_flow.py Wrap flow body with mlflow.start_run() context Log plugin results with post_ prefix Call FlowContract.log_flow_completion() at end
task.acceptance_criteria: - MLflow run created for each post_training_flow() invocation - Plugin metrics visible in MLflow UI with post_ prefix - upstream_training_run_id tag links to training run

phase.name: Analysis, Deployment, Dashboard, QA — Volume and Contract Wiring

task.title: Wire FlowContract into analysis_flow — add flow_name tag and log_flow_completion
task.current_state: analysis_flow.py opens MLflow runs (ExperimentTracker is used). However: - The flow does not read upstream training run ID via FlowContract - The flow does not tag itself with flow_name="analyze" - FlowContract.log_flow_completion() is never called - Output figures/tables go to relative paths (not /app/outputs/analysis from env var)
task.target_state: analysis_flow(): 1. Reads upstream training run ID via FlowContract.find_upstream_run("minivess_training", "train") 2. Tags its MLflow run with upstream_training_run_id and flow_name="analyze" 3. Writes output figures to Path(os.environ.get("ANALYSIS_OUTPUT", "/app/outputs/analysis")) 4. Calls FlowContract.log_flow_completion(flow_name="analyze", ...) 5. Returns analysis result with mlflow_run_id included
task.tdd_spec: RED phase — tests/v2/unit/test_analysis_flow_contract.py: test_analysis_flow_tags_upstream_run: MLflow run has "upstream_training_run_id" tag test_analysis_flow_sets_flow_name_tag: MLflow run has tags["flow_name"] == "analyze" test_analysis_output_dir_from_env: with ANALYSIS_OUTPUT=/test/analysis, figures are written to /test/analysis/ test_analysis_no_hardcoded_relative_outputs: ast.parse() finds no Path("outputs/analysis") literals in analysis_flow.py GREEN phase: Add FlowContract import and wiring at start of analysis_flow() Replace hardcoded output paths with ANALYSIS_OUTPUT env var Add FlowContract.log_flow_completion() call at end
task.acceptance_criteria: - Analysis MLflow runs link to upstream training runs via tag - Output directory is env-var-controlled - FlowContract.log_flow_completion() is called for every successful analysis run

task.title: Fix deploy_flow output paths and add BentoML store volume wiring
task.current_state: deploy_flow.py: - onnx_dir = config.output_dir / "onnx" — depends on DeployConfig.output_dir - DeployConfig default output_dir may not be /app/outputs/deploy - BentoML model store path is not explicitly configured — uses BentoML default (~/.bentoml inside the container, not a mounted volume) - deploy service in docker-compose.flows.yml has ZERO volume mounts
task.target_state: - DeployConfig.output_dir defaults to Path(os.environ.get("DEPLOY_OUTPUT_DIR", "/app/outputs/deploy")) - BENTOML_HOME env var is set to /home/minivess/bentoml (mapped to bentoml_store volume) - deploy service has volume mounts for mlruns (ro), outputs_deploy (rw), bentoml_store (rw) - FlowContract.log_flow_completion() called at end of deploy_flow() - deploy_flow() tags MLflow run with flow_name="deploy" and upstream_analysis_run_id
task.tdd_spec: RED phase — tests/v2/unit/test_deploy_flow_paths.py: test_deploy_output_dir_from_env: DeployConfig with DEPLOY_OUTPUT_DIR=/test/deploy uses Path("/test/deploy") as output root test_deploy_onnx_dir_within_output: onnx_dir is output_dir / "onnx" (absolute) test_deploy_bentoml_home_from_env: BentoML import uses BENTOML_HOME env var test_deploy_flow_no_relative_paths: ast.parse() finds no Path("outputs/deploy") literals GREEN phase: Update src/minivess/config/deploy_config.py DeployConfig.output_dir default Add BENTOML_HOME env var wiring to deploy_flow() Add FlowContract wiring Update docker-compose.flows.yml deploy service (covered in T-04)
task.acceptance_criteria: - ONNX files persisted to outputs_deploy volume after deploy_flow() - BentoML models in bentoml_store volume (not in container-ephemeral ~/.bentoml) - deploy service in docker-compose.flows.yml has at least 3 volume mounts

task.title: Fix dashboard_flow output paths and add FlowContract wiring
task.current_state: dashboard_flow.py run_dashboard_flow(output_dir: Path, ...): - output_dir is a required parameter — caller must provide it - No default that reads from DASHBOARD_OUTPUT env var - No FlowContract wiring - dashboard service in docker-compose.flows.yml has ZERO volume mounts
task.target_state: - run_dashboard_flow accepts output_dir with default: Path(os.environ.get("DASHBOARD_OUTPUT", "/app/outputs/dashboard")) - dashboard service has volume mounts for mlruns (ro), outputs_analysis (ro), outputs_dashboard (rw) - FlowContract reads from any upstream completed flow (analyze or deploy) - FlowContract.log_flow_completion() called at end
task.tdd_spec: RED phase — tests/v2/unit/test_dashboard_flow_paths.py: test_dashboard_output_from_env: with DASHBOARD_OUTPUT=/test/dash, report written to /test/dash/ test_dashboard_creates_report: run_dashboard_flow() creates everything_dashboard_report.md test_dashboard_report_not_in_tmp: report_path does not start with /tmp test_dashboard_metadata_json_created: everything_dashboard_metadata.json is created GREEN phase: Update run_dashboard_flow() output_dir default Add FlowContract wiring Update docker-compose.flows.yml dashboard service (covered in T-04)
task.acceptance_criteria: - Dashboard report persisted to outputs_dashboard volume after run_dashboard_flow() - DASHBOARD_OUTPUT env var controls output location - Report and JSON files created at the end of a successful flow run

task.title: Fix qa_flow tracking URI to use MLFLOW_TRACKING_URI env var and add report persistence
task.current_state: qa_flow.py: qa_flow(tracking_uri: str = "mlruns", ...): - Default "mlruns" is a relative path — in Docker resolves to /app/mlruns - /app/mlruns has no volume mount → qa_flow sees an empty mlruns directory - QA report is returned as a string but never persisted to disk - qa service in docker-compose.flows.yml has ZERO volume mounts
task.target_state: qa_flow(): - Default tracking_uri reads from MLFLOW_TRACKING_URI env var (which is "http://minivess-mlflow:5000" in Docker context — the server backend) - QA report is written to Path(os.environ.get("DASHBOARD_OUTPUT", "/app/outputs/dashboard")) / "qa_report.md" - MLflow QA results (ghost run count, param violations) logged as tags via mlflow.set_tag() - qa service has volume mount for outputs_dashboard (rw) and mlruns (ro)
task.tdd_spec: RED phase — tests/v2/unit/test_qa_flow_uri.py: test_qa_uses_env_tracking_uri: with MLFLOW_TRACKING_URI=http://test:5000 set, qa_flow() uses "http://test:5000" not "mlruns" test_qa_report_written_to_disk: qa_flow() writes qa_report.md to DASHBOARD_OUTPUT dir test_qa_report_path_absolute: qa report path is absolute (not relative) test_qa_flow_on_empty_mlruns_does_not_crash: qa_flow() with empty tracking store returns summary with 0 ghost runs (not exception) GREEN phase: Update qa_flow() default tracking_uri to read from os.environ Add report file write at end of qa_flow() Update docker-compose.flows.yml qa service (covered in T-04)
task.acceptance_criteria: - MLFLOW_TRACKING_URI env var controls tracking backend for qa_flow() - QA report written to disk at env-configured path - qa_flow() does not crash when MLflow server is unreachable (returns warning summary)

phase.name: Acquisition Flow — Docker Service and MLflow Logging

task.title: Add acquisition service to docker-compose.flows.yml with raw_data volume mount
task.current_state: acquisition_flow.py is well-implemented with @flow decorator and proper task structure. Dockerfile.acquisition exists at deployment/docker/Dockerfile.acquisition. deployments.py has "acquisition" in FLOW_WORK_POOL_MAP and FLOW_IMAGE_MAP. BUT: deployment/docker-compose.flows.yml has NO "acquisition" service definition. The flow has no Docker infrastructure for running via compose.
task.target_state: docker-compose.flows.yml acquisition service: build: deployment/docker/Dockerfile.acquisition image: minivess-acquisition:latest container_name: minivess-flow-acquisition environment: MLFLOW_TRACKING_URI: http://minivess-mlflow:5000 PREFECT_API_URL: http://minivess-prefect:4200/api ACQUISITION_OUTPUT_DIR: /app/data/raw FLOW_NAME: acquisition volumes: - raw_data:/app/data/raw - logs_data:/app/logs networks: - minivess
task.tdd_spec: RED phase — extend tests/v2/integration/test_docker_compose_volumes.py (from T-04): test_acquisition_service_has_raw_data_volume (already in T-04 spec) test_acquisition_image_correct: services["acquisition"]["image"] == "minivess-acquisition:latest" test_acquisition_env_has_output_dir: "ACQUISITION_OUTPUT_DIR" in services["acquisition"]["environment"] test_raw_data_volume_declared: "raw_data" in compose_config["volumes"] GREEN phase: Add acquisition service to docker-compose.flows.yml Add raw_data and logs_data to volumes section if not already added in T-04
task.acceptance_criteria: - docker compose -f deployment/docker-compose.flows.yml config includes acquisition service - acquisition service has raw_data volume mount - raw_data named volume is declared
task.files_to_modify: deployment/docker-compose.flows.yml (acquisition service addition — may be done in T-04) tests/v2/integration/test_docker_compose_volumes.py (extend)

task.title: Add MLflow run logging to acquisition_flow — log acq_ params to minivess_acquisition experiment
task.current_state: acquisition_flow.py log_acquisition_provenance_task() returns a dict with acq_ prefixed params, but never logs them to MLflow. No MLflow run is opened in run_acquisition_flow(). The provenance data is returned to the caller but not persisted.
task.target_state: run_acquisition_flow(): 1. Opens MLflow run in "minivess_acquisition" experiment 2. Logs acq_n_datasets, acq_total_volumes, acq_timestamp, acq_statuses to MLflow 3. Tags run with flow_name="acquisition" 4. Calls FlowContract.log_flow_completion() 5. AcquisitionResult includes mlflow_run_id field
task.tdd_spec: RED phase — tests/v2/unit/test_acquisition_mlflow.py: test_acquisition_opens_mlflow_run: after run_acquisition_flow() with test config, mlflow.search_runs("minivess_acquisition") returns at least 1 run test_acquisition_logs_acq_params: MLflow run params include "acq_n_datasets" test_acquisition_flow_name_tag: MLflow run tags["flow_name"] == "acquisition" test_acquisition_result_has_run_id: AcquisitionResult.mlflow_run_id is not None GREEN phase: Add MLflow run opening to run_acquisition_flow() Add mlflow_run_id field to AcquisitionResult dataclass Add FlowContract wiring
task.acceptance_criteria: - MLflow run created in "minivess_acquisition" experiment per flow invocation - Provenance params visible in MLflow UI with acq_ prefix

task.title: Implement epoch_latest.yaml writer in SegmentationTrainer.fit() loop
task.current_state: src/minivess/pipeline/trainer.py: - Saves best-metric checkpoints on improvement (lines 568-591) - Saves metric_history.json - Does NOT write epoch_latest.yaml - Does NOT write a resume-from-any-epoch checkpoint (epoch_latest.pth) - No SIGTERM handler - The concept "epoch_latest.yaml" does not exist anywhere in the codebase
task.tdd_spec: RED phase — tests/v2/unit/test_epoch_latest_artifact.py: test_epoch_latest_yaml_written: after trainer.fit() for 1 epoch with mock data, checkpoint_dir / "epoch_latest.yaml" exists test_epoch_latest_yaml_parseable: yaml.safe_load(epoch_latest.yaml content) returns dict with keys: epoch, fold, mlflow_run_id, best_val_loss, timestamp test_epoch_latest_yaml_no_regex: epoch is int (not str), fold is int, mlflow_run_id is str test_epoch_latest_yaml_epoch_increments: after 3 epochs, epoch_latest.yaml["epoch"] == 2 test_epoch_latest_pth_written: checkpoint_dir / "epoch_latest.pth" exists test_epoch_latest_pth_loadable: torch.load(epoch_latest.pth) is a dict (state_dict) test_epoch_latest_yaml_utc_timestamp: timestamp in epoch_latest.yaml ends with "+00:00" or "Z" GREEN phase: Add epoch_latest_path = checkpoint_dir / "epoch_latest.yaml" in SegmentationTrainer.fit() Write after each epoch using: import yaml state = {"epoch": epoch, "fold": fold_id, "mlflow_run_id": tracker.run_id, ...} epoch_latest_path.write_text(yaml.dump(state), encoding="utf-8") Save epoch_latest.pth after each epoch Add signal.signal(signal.SIGTERM, lambda *_: save_and_exit()) handler
task.acceptance_criteria: - epoch_latest.yaml exists after each training epoch - yaml.safe_load() can parse it without error - epoch number in YAML matches the completed epoch (not off-by-one) - SIGTERM causes graceful write of epoch_latest.yaml before exit

task.title: Implement check_resume_state_task in training_flow — read epoch_latest.yaml with yaml.safe_load()
task.current_state: training_flow() (post T-06) will call SegmentationTrainer.fit() but does not check for existing epoch_latest.yaml before starting. Every invocation starts from epoch 0. There is no resume detection logic.
task.target_state:
@task(name="check-resume-state") def check_resume_state_task(checkpoint_dir: Path) -> dict | None: """Check for epoch_latest.yaml to determine if this is a resumed run. Uses yaml.safe_load() — NO regex. """ latest_path = checkpoint_dir / "epoch_latest.yaml" if not latest_path.exists(): return None import yaml with latest_path.open(encoding="utf-8") as f: state = yaml.safe_load(f) # Validate the referenced MLflow run is RUNNING import mlflow run = mlflow.get_run(state["mlflow_run_id"]) if run.info.status == "RUNNING": return state return None # Stale state from a completed or failed run
training_flow() calls check_resume_state_task() before starting training. If resume state found: resumes from epoch_latest + 1, reuses existing MLflow run. If not found: starts fresh MLflow run.
task.tdd_spec: RED phase — tests/v2/unit/test_resume_detection.py: test_no_resume_when_no_yaml: check_resume_state_task(empty_dir) returns None test_resume_detected_when_yaml_exists: with epoch_latest.yaml present, check_resume_state_task() returns dict with "epoch" key test_resume_yaml_parsed_with_yaml_safe_load: mock confirms yaml.safe_load called, not ast.literal_eval or any regex test_stale_state_ignored: if MLflow run status is FINISHED, returns None test_running_state_resumable: if MLflow run status is RUNNING, returns state dict test_training_flow_resumes_from_epoch: training_flow() called with existing epoch_latest.yaml (epoch=5), starts training from epoch 6 (not epoch 0) GREEN phase: Add check_resume_state_task() to train_flow.py Wire into training_flow() before fold loop Update fold training to accept start_epoch parameter
task.acceptance_criteria: - check_resume_state_task() uses yaml.safe_load() exclusively for YAML parsing - NO import re or regex in check_resume_state_task() - training_flow() resumes from correct epoch when epoch_latest.yaml is present and run is RUNNING - Epoch-level metrics in resumed run continue from the correct step number

phase.name: Shell Entry Points and Prefect Deployment Registration

task.title: Create scripts/run_training.sh — shell wrapper calling prefect deployment run
task.current_state: No shell script exists that wraps a Prefect flow invocation. The only shell scripts available call Python scripts directly — banned. train_alternative_variants.sh (from recent commits) calls python scripts — banned.
task.target_state:
scripts/run_training.sh: #!/usr/bin/env bash set -euo pipefail
LOSS="${1:-cbdice_cldice}" MODEL="${2:-dynunet}" COMPUTE="${3:-auto}" EXPERIMENT="${4:-dynunet_loss_variation}"
echo "Submitting training flow: loss=${LOSS} model=${MODEL}" prefect deployment run 'training-flow/default' \ --params "{\"loss_name\": \"${LOSS}\", \"model_family\": \"${MODEL}\", \"compute\": \"${COMPUTE}\", \"experiment_name\": \"${EXPERIMENT}\"}"
scripts/run_pipeline.sh: Wraps the full pipeline trigger chain via Prefect deployments.
scripts/run_analysis.sh: Wraps analysis flow invocation.
All scripts: executable bit set (chmod +x), shebang #!/usr/bin/env bash, set -euo pipefail, no python scripts/*.py invocations.
task.tdd_spec: RED phase — tests/v2/unit/test_shell_scripts.py: test_run_training_sh_no_python_script: open("scripts/run_training.sh").read() does not contain "python scripts/" test_run_training_sh_has_prefect_run: "prefect deployment run" in script content test_run_training_sh_executable: os.access("scripts/run_training.sh", os.X_OK) is True test_run_pipeline_sh_no_python_script: same for run_pipeline.sh test_run_analysis_sh_no_python_script: same for run_analysis.sh NOTE: All file reads use open().read() — no subprocess invocation in tests GREEN phase: Create scripts/run_training.sh with prefect deployment run Create scripts/run_pipeline.sh Create scripts/run_analysis.sh chmod +x all scripts
task.acceptance_criteria: - scripts/run_training.sh, run_pipeline.sh, run_analysis.sh exist and are executable - None contain "python scripts/*.py" or "uv run python scripts/" - All use "prefect deployment run" as the execution primitive - Scripts accept command-line arguments for key parameters

task.title: Add deprecation warning to train_monitored.py — make it non-invocable as primary entry point
task.current_state: scripts/train_monitored.py is a fully functional standalone training script. It has no indication that it is not the supported entry point. It is documented in its own docstring as the way to run training: "Usage: uv run python scripts/train_monitored.py ..." The script remains needed for migration reference and CI-mode (PREFECT_DISABLED=1).
task.target_state:
At the top of scripts/train_monitored.py __main__ block, add: import warnings warnings.warn( "

" "DEPRECATED ENTRY POINT: scripts/train_monitored.py
" "This script is NOT the supported way to run training.
" "The correct entry point is:
" " prefect deployment run 'training-flow/default' --params '{...}'
" "Or use: scripts/run_training.sh <loss> <model>
" "This script will be removed in a future release.
" "Set ALLOW_STANDALONE_TRAINING=1 to suppress this warning.", DeprecationWarning, stacklevel=2, ) if not os.environ.get("ALLOW_STANDALONE_TRAINING"): sys.exit(1) Update docstring to remove "Usage:" examples that show direct invocation.
task.tdd_spec: RED phase — tests/v2/unit/test_train_monitored_deprecation.py: test_train_monitored_warns_without_env: without ALLOW_STANDALONE_TRAINING, importing and running __main__ raises SystemExit(1) test_train_monitored_allowed_with_env: with ALLOW_STANDALONE_TRAINING=1, the script continues past the deprecation gate test_deprecation_warning_is_deprecationwarning: the warning category is DeprecationWarning GREEN phase: Add deprecation gate to scripts/train_monitored.py __main__ block Update docstring to remove direct invocation examples
task.acceptance_criteria: - Running scripts/train_monitored.py without ALLOW_STANDALONE_TRAINING=1 exits with error - Error message clearly states the correct entry point - CI tests that use train_monitored.py set ALLOW_STANDALONE_TRAINING=1 explicitly
task.files_to_modify: scripts/train_monitored.py (deprecation gate in __main__) tests/v2/unit/test_train_monitored_deprecation.py (new) Any CI scripts that invoke train_monitored.py (add ALLOW_STANDALONE_TRAINING=1)

task.title: Create deployment/prefect/deployments.yaml with all 9 flows defined
task.current_state: deployment/prefect/ directory contains only work-pools.yaml. No deployments.yaml exists. Flows are not registered with Prefect Server. `prefect deployment run 'training-flow/default'` would fail because no deployment named "default" exists for "training-flow".
task.target_state:
deployment/prefect/deployments.yaml (Prefect 3.x deployment YAML or Python serve): deployments for all 9 flows: - minivess-acquisition/default → cpu-pool - minivess-data/default → cpu-pool - training-flow/default → gpu-pool - Post-Training Pipeline/default → cpu-pool - analysis-flow/default → cpu-pool (name from @flow decorator) - Deploy Pipeline/default → cpu-pool - minivess-dashboard/default → cpu-pool - qa-flow/default → cpu-pool - minivess-annotation/default → cpu-pool
OR: src/minivess/orchestration/serve.py (Python serve API): from prefect.deployments import Deployment Deployment.build_from_flow(training_flow, name="default", work_pool_name="gpu-pool") (etc. for all flows)
Either approach must result in `prefect deployment run` working for all flows.
task.tdd_spec: RED phase — tests/v2/unit/test_deployments_yaml.py: test_deployments_yaml_parseable: yaml.safe_load(deployments.yaml) returns dict test_all_flows_have_deployment: all 9 flow names appear in deployments YAML test_train_flow_on_gpu_pool: training-flow deployment uses work_pool_name == "gpu-pool" test_cpu_flows_on_cpu_pool: all non-train flows use work_pool_name == "cpu-pool" NOTE: YAML parsing uses yaml.safe_load() — no regex GREEN phase: Create deployment/prefect/deployments.yaml OR create src/minivess/orchestration/serve.py Register all 9 flows with correct work pools
task.acceptance_criteria: - deployment/prefect/deployments.yaml exists and is valid YAML - All 9 flows have a "default" deployment registered - training-flow/default uses gpu-pool - All other deployments use cpu-pool - `prefect deployment ls` shows all 9 deployments after registration

task.title: Verify MLflow epoch-level curve logging works end-to-end in training_flow()
task.current_state:
ExperimentTracker.log_epoch_metrics() at tracking.py:209 uses mlflow.log_metrics(step=step). SegmentationTrainer.fit() calls self.tracker.log_epoch_metrics(epoch_log, step=epoch+1). This works in train_monitored.py but is NOT wired into training_flow() (which is a stub).
After T-06 implements real training_flow(), this must be verified end-to-end: epoch-level curves must appear in MLflow with correct step numbers.
task.target_state: After training_flow(debug=True, max_epochs=3) completes: - MLflow run in "minivess_training" has val_loss metric at step=1, step=2, step=3 - train_loss metric appears at step=1, step=2, step=3 - sys_gpu_* metrics appear at each step (if GPU available) or are absent (CPU) - learning_rate metric appears at each step The MLflow UI shows smooth learning curves for the run.
task.tdd_spec: RED phase — tests/v2/integration/test_epoch_curve_logging.py: test_val_loss_at_each_epoch: after training_flow(debug=True, max_epochs=3, num_folds=1), run = mlflow.search_runs("minivess_training")[-1] history = mlflow.MlflowClient().get_metric_history(run.info.run_id, "val_loss") assert len(history) == 3 assert history[0].step == 1 and history[1].step == 2 and history[2].step == 3 test_train_loss_at_each_epoch: same check for "train_loss" test_learning_rate_logged: "learning_rate" in run.data.metrics test_epoch_curves_monotone_in_step: steps are sequential integers starting at 1 GREEN phase: Ensure training_flow() (post T-06) correctly passes tracker to SegmentationTrainer Verify tracker has the active MLflow run ID before training starts Verify no step reset between epochs
task.acceptance_criteria: - MLflow run has val_loss history with exactly max_epochs entries - Steps are sequential starting at 1 - MLflow UI renders learning curves (not flat lines or single points)

phase.name: Data Flow — DVC Integration and Cloud-Agnostic Storage

task.title: Add dvc_pull_task to data_flow.py — cloud-agnostic DVC remote configuration
task.current_state: data_flow.py does NOT use DVC. It calls discover_external_test_pairs() directly on the filesystem. There is no dvc pull invocation, no DVC cache, and no version tracking. The "DVC versioning as inter-flow contract" is entirely absent.
task.tdd_spec: RED phase — tests/v2/unit/test_dvc_pull_task.py: test_dvc_pull_task_returns_commit_hash: with mock subprocess, returns a hex string test_dvc_pull_task_passes_rev: with dvc_rev="abc123", subprocess cmd includes "--rev abc123" test_dvc_pull_task_passes_remote: with remote="s3", subprocess cmd includes "--remote s3" test_data_flow_logs_dvc_commit: MLflow run from run_data_flow() has "data_dvc_commit" param test_dvc_remotes_yaml_parseable: yaml.safe_load(configs/dvc/remotes.yaml) returns dict with "remotes" key containing local, s3, gcs, azure entries NOTE: YAML parsing with yaml.safe_load() — no regex GREEN phase: Add dvc_pull_task() to data_flow.py Update run_data_flow() signature and MLflow logging Create configs/dvc/remotes.yaml
task.acceptance_criteria: - dvc_pull_task() invocable with mock DVC environment - data_dvc_commit param logged to MLflow in data engineering runs - configs/dvc/remotes.yaml covers local, S3 (MinIO), GCS, Azure backends

task.title: Add MLflow run logging to data_flow — log data_ params to minivess_data experiment
task.current_state: data_flow.py log_data_provenance_task() computes provenance dict with data_ prefix but does NOT open an MLflow run. The provenance is returned to the caller but never persisted to MLflow. No downstream flow can discover the data engineering run via FlowContract.find_upstream_run("minivess_data", "data").
task.target_state: run_data_flow(): 1. Opens MLflow run in "minivess_data" experiment 2. Logs data_n_volumes, data_n_folds, data_hash, (data_dvc_commit if T-23 done) 3. Tags run with flow_name="data" 4. Calls FlowContract.log_flow_completion() 5. DataFlowResult includes mlflow_run_id field
task.tdd_spec: RED phase — tests/v2/unit/test_data_flow_mlflow.py: test_data_flow_opens_mlflow_run: after run_data_flow() with minimal data, mlflow.search_runs("minivess_data") returns at least 1 run test_data_flow_logs_n_volumes: MLflow run params["data_n_volumes"] == str(n_pairs) test_data_flow_logs_hash: MLflow run params include "data_hash" (16-char hex string) test_data_flow_flow_name_tag: MLflow run tags["flow_name"] == "data" test_data_flow_result_has_run_id: DataFlowResult.mlflow_run_id is not None GREEN phase: Add ExperimentTracker and FlowContract imports to data_flow.py Open MLflow run in run_data_flow() Add mlflow_run_id to DataFlowResult
task.acceptance_criteria: - MLflow run created in "minivess_data" experiment per flow invocation - Training flow can discover data run via FlowContract.find_upstream_run() - data_n_volumes and data_hash params visible in MLflow UI

task.title: Create Dockerfile.annotation and add annotation service to docker-compose.flows.yml
task.current_state: annotation_flow.py exists with @flow decorator and real implementation. BUT: No Dockerfile.annotation exists. No annotation service in docker-compose.flows.yml. Not in FLOW_WORK_POOL_MAP (fixed in T-03) but still no Docker infrastructure. The flow cannot be run in Docker context — only directly in Python.
task.target_state:
deployment/docker/Dockerfile.annotation: FROM minivess-base:latest LABEL flow="annotation" COPY scripts/ scripts/ CMD ["python", "-m", "minivess.orchestration.flows.annotation_flow"]
docker-compose.flows.yml annotation service: volumes: - bentoml_store:/home/minivess/bentoml:ro (read BentoML models) - data_cache:/app/data:ro (input volumes) - mlruns_data:/app/mlruns (log annotation sessions) environment: BENTOML_HOME: /home/minivess/bentoml BENTO_SERVER_URL: http://minivess-bentoml:3000 (or empty for local inference)
task.tdd_spec: RED phase — extend tests/v2/integration/test_docker_compose_volumes.py: test_annotation_service_exists: "annotation" in yaml.safe_load(compose)["services"] test_annotation_has_bentoml_mount: bentoml_store volume in annotation volumes test_dockerfile_annotation_exists: Path("deployment/docker/Dockerfile.annotation").exists() GREEN phase: Create deployment/docker/Dockerfile.annotation Add annotation service to docker-compose.flows.yml (covered in T-04 scope or separately)
task.acceptance_criteria: - Dockerfile.annotation exists and is valid - annotation service in docker-compose.flows.yml with bentoml_store mount - docker compose -f deployment/docker-compose.flows.yml build annotation exits 0

phase.name: Split File Serialization — Persist Fold Splits to Volume

task.title: Serialize fold splits to JSON in data_flow — write to configs_splits volume
task.current_state: data_flow.py split_data_task() returns list[FoldSplit] as Python objects. These objects are returned in DataFlowResult.splits. There is NO serialization of splits to disk. There is NO volume mount for configs_splits in the data service. The training flow cannot read splits from a file — it would need to call data_flow directly, which violates the no-direct-function-calls rule.
task.target_state:
@task(name="serialize-splits") def serialize_splits_task(splits: list[FoldSplit], splits_dir: Path) -> Path: """Serialize FoldSplit list to JSON at splits_dir/splits.json. Returns path to the splits file. Uses json.dumps() — no regex. """ splits_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True) splits_path = splits_dir / "splits.json" import json serialized = [ {"fold_id": s.fold_id, "train": [str(p["image"]) for p in s.train], "val": [str(p["image"]) for p in s.val]} for s in splits ] splits_path.write_text(json.dumps(serialized, indent=2), encoding="utf-8") return splits_path
run_data_flow() calls serialize_splits_task() after split_data_task(). splits_dir defaults to Path(os.environ.get("SPLITS_OUTPUT_DIR", "/app/configs/splits")) DataFlowResult includes splits_path field.
task.tdd_spec: RED phase — tests/v2/unit/test_splits_serialization.py: test_splits_written_to_json: after run_data_flow() with mock pairs, splits.json exists test_splits_json_parseable: json.loads(splits.json content) returns list of dicts test_splits_json_has_fold_id: each dict in splits list has "fold_id" key test_splits_json_has_train_val: each dict has "train" and "val" lists test_splits_path_from_env: with SPLITS_OUTPUT_DIR=/test/splits, splits written to /test/splits/ test_training_flow_reads_splits: training_flow() with SPLITS_DIR=/test/splits, mock splits.json present, flow reads it via json.loads() (not yaml, not regex) GREEN phase: Add serialize_splits_task() to data_flow.py Wire into run_data_flow() after split_data_task() Add SPLITS_OUTPUT_DIR env var reading Update training_flow() (post T-06) to read splits from SPLITS_DIR/splits.json
task.acceptance_criteria: - splits.json written to SPLITS_OUTPUT_DIR after data flow completes - json.loads() can parse the file without error - training_flow() reads splits from splits.json (not from Python DataFlowResult object) - No regex in splits serialization or deserialization

task.title: Write integration test: data_flow → training_flow artifact handoff via volumes
task.current_state: No integration test verifies the data → training flow handoff. The only verified handoff was via Python function calls (not Docker volumes). Volume-based handoff (splits.json + data_cache) has never been tested.
task.target_state: tests/v2/integration/test_data_to_train_handoff.py: test_data_flow_writes_splits_readable_by_train: 1. Create temp dirs for SPLITS_OUTPUT_DIR and DATA_DIR 2. Run run_data_flow() with mock data and SPLITS_OUTPUT_DIR set 3. Assert splits.json written to SPLITS_OUTPUT_DIR 4. Set SPLITS_DIR = SPLITS_OUTPUT_DIR 5. Run training_flow(debug=True, max_epochs=1, num_folds=1) 6. Assert training ran (MLflow run exists, checkpoint written) 7. Assert training read from splits.json (not hardcoded) NOTE: uses actual file I/O via tmp_path pytest fixture, not mocks
task.tdd_spec: The test itself is the RED phase (it will fail until T-06 and T-26 are done). Run after T-06 and T-26 are complete to verify end-to-end.
task.acceptance_criteria: - Integration test passes after T-06 and T-26 are complete - test verifies actual file I/O, not just Python object passing - test verifies MLflow run created by training_flow has correct data provenance

task.title: Write integration test: all flows produce non-ephemeral outputs — no /tmp artifacts
task.current_state: No automated test verifies that flow outputs go to mounted volumes, not /tmp. The only evidence of correct behavior is manual inspection after container runs.
task.target_state: tests/v2/integration/test_no_ephemeral_outputs.py: test_train_flow_checkpoint_not_in_tmp: Run training_flow(debug=True, max_epochs=1, num_folds=1) with CHECKPOINT_DIR=/test/ckpt Assert all .pth files are under /test/ckpt, NOT under /tmp test_post_training_outputs_not_in_tmp: Run post_training_flow() with POST_TRAINING_OUTPUT_DIR=/test/pt Assert all plugin outputs are under /test/pt test_analysis_outputs_not_in_tmp: Run analysis_flow() with ANALYSIS_OUTPUT=/test/analysis Assert all figures/tables are under /test/analysis test_deploy_outputs_not_in_tmp: Run deploy_flow() with DEPLOY_OUTPUT_DIR=/test/deploy Assert ONNX files and bentofiles are under /test/deploy test_dashboard_outputs_not_in_tmp: Run run_dashboard_flow() with DASHBOARD_OUTPUT=/test/dashboard Assert report and JSON under /test/dashboard
task.tdd_spec: Tests are RED until all preceding phase tasks are complete. After T-05, T-06, T-09, T-11, T-12, T-13: run the tests to verify. Tests use os.walk() over /tmp and each env-var-configured output dir. No regex in path checking — use pathlib.Path.is_relative_to() or str.startswith()
task.acceptance_criteria: - All test assertions pass - No artifact produced by any flow resides under /tmp after flow completion - Every flow writes outputs to its env-var-configured path

constraints.constraint: import re is BANNED — use ast.parse, json.loads, yaml.safe_load, str.split, str.partition, str.rsplit, pathlib.Path attributes
/tmp and tempfile.mkdtemp() are BANNED for artifacts that must survive container exit — use CHECKPOINT_DIR env var
python scripts/*.py is BANNED as training entry point — use prefect deployment run or a shell script wrapping it
All Docker container outputs must be on named volumes — no ephemeral writes
MLflow is the ONLY inter-flow contract — no shared filesystem, no direct function calls between containers
from __future__ import annotations at top of every Python file
pathlib.Path() always — never string concatenation for paths
TDD mandatory — tests FIRST, implementation SECOND, ALWAYS
uv ONLY — never pip, conda, requirements.txt
No placeholder implementations — pass, TODO, NotImplementedError banned in production code
Hard-fail on missing required components — RuntimeError with actionable multi-line error message, never silent fallback
Never confabulate — web-search or ask user when uncertain
